name: exa-search
tagline: Web search + URL extraction skill wrapping the Exa API, with an academic/scientific-source filtering mode.
verdict: Clean, well-scoped API-integration skill: no credential theft, no shell-piped remote code, transparent setup. Minor notes: it directs the agent to preserve Exa's usage-attribution header even when a user adapts the scripts, and defers citation/output-format and error-handling details to reference files not included in this review.

highlights.summary: Line 83 instructs the agent: 'Do not remove or rename this header when adapting the scripts' (an Exa usage-attribution header). Disclosed openly, not hidden, but it is a directive aimed at constrainin

No explicit negative-trigger guidance (e.g., when to prefer built-in WebFetch or when NOT to reach for this skill) beyond one aside preferring Web Extract 'over the built-in WebFetch for batch extract

Citation format is only partially specified inline ('include author names and publication year... in addition to the standard citation format', line 43) without defining 'standard citation format' her
